searchDocs.title: SpecificLocation​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SpecificLocation { line: number column: number }

searchDocs.title: BroadLocation​
searchDocs.content: </> interface BroadLocation { notKnown: boolean? begin: SpecificLocation end_: SpecificLocation }

searchDocs.title: isErrors​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauError.isErrors(tree: {any}) → boolean Checks if a given AST has errors.

searchDocs.title: error​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauError.error(tree: {any}) → () Throws an error with a formatted message if the given AST has errors.

searchDocs.title: CustomError​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauError.CustomError( errs: {ErrorTable}, msg: string? ) → typeof(Stringbuilder.new())

interface ErrorTable { message: string location: BroadLocation }  Allows you to create a custom error message with a custom label, and also allows you to pass in multiple errors to be displayed in the same message.

searchDocs.title: raise​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:raise( message: string,-- the error text location: Location?-- defaults to the node currently being evaluated ) → ()

searchDocs.title: evalExpr​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:evalExpr( node: any, scope: Scope ) → ...any

searchDocs.title: execStat​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:execStat( node: any, scope: Scope ) → Completion

searchDocs.title: evalExprList​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:evalExprList( nodes: {any}, scope: Scope ) → {any}-- an array (.n)

searchDocs.title: assign​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:assign( target: any,-- an ExprLocal/ExprGlobal/ExprIndexName/ExprIndexExpr node value: any,-- the value to store scope: Scope ) → ()

searchDocs.title: makeClosure​
searchDocs.content: </> Runtime:makeClosure( funcNode: any,-- an AstExprFunction node defScope: Scope-- the lexical scope the closure captures ) → (...any) → ...any Build a closure from an AstExprFunction

searchDocs.title: declare​
searchDocs.content: </> Scope:declare( name: string, value: any ) → () Declares a new local variable in a scope. Even if a var exists with the same name elsewhere it will be re-declared.

searchDocs.title: get​
searchDocs.content: </> Scope:get(name: string) → ( value: any, found: boolean ) Searches for a given variable and returns it's value, and also searches all elevated scopes. If no variable of that name exists, returns nil and found=false

searchDocs.title: set​
searchDocs.content: </> Scope:set( name: string, value: any ) → successboolean Sets a given variable to a new value. Returns false if the variable doesn't exist.

searchDocs.title: Stringbuilder​
searchDocs.content: </> type Stringbuilder = typeof(Stringbuilder.new()) The instance type returned by Stringbuilder.new().

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.new() → Stringbuilder Returns a new Stringbuilder. Based on the C# implementation.

searchDocs.title: returnString​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.returnString() → string Returns the string built by the Stringbuilder. Same as calling tostring() on the Stringbuilder object.

searchDocs.title: tostring​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.tostring() → string Returns the string built by the Stringbuilder. Same as calling tostring() on the Stringbuilder object.

searchDocs.title: append​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.append(str: string) → () Appends a string to the end of the current Stringbuilder's string.

searchDocs.title: appendLn​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.appendLn(str: string) → () Appends a string to the end of the current Stringbuilder's string, with a newline at the end.

searchDocs.title: appendLnBefore​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.appendLnBefore(str: string) → () Appends a string to the end of the current Stringbuilder's string on a new line.

searchDocs.title: appendNewLine​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.appendNewLine() → () Appends a newline to the end of the current Stringbuilder's string.

searchDocs.title: appendNewLines​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.appendNewLines(n: number) → () Appends the specified amount of newlines to the current Stringbuilder's string.

searchDocs.title: clear​
searchDocs.content: </> Stringbuilder.clear() → () Delete's the current Stringbuilder's stored string. You can use :returnString() or :tostring() to get the string before clearing it.

searchDocs.title: Completion​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Completion { kind: "Normal" | "Return" | "Break" | "Continue" values: {any}?-- only present if kind == "Return" } Describes how a statement or a whole LuauDispatcher.run call finished.

searchDocs.title: RuntimeOptions​
searchDocs.content: </> interface RuntimeOptions { rawGlobalEnabled: boolean?-- Allows scripts to use the rawglobal(name, value) function. overrideCertainGlobalsAtRuntime: boolean?-- Overrides error, assert, warn, print, and various methods from game:GetService("TestService") noEnv: boolean?-- Runs the script without libs and functions from Dispatcher.globals instructionLimit: number?-- The maximum number of expressions/statements evaluated before stopping script execution (default 10,000,000). allowEnvironmentModifications: boolean?-- Allows scripts to overwrite globals in the environment. allowConsoleOutput: boolean?-- If true, output functions will be able to print to the Roblox console. }

searchDocs.title: RunningCount​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> LuauDispatcher.RunningCount: number The number of ASTs currently executing inside LuauDispatcher.run.

searchDocs.title: RunningStatusChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.RunningStatusChanged: ScriptSignal<boolean> Dispatcher.RunningStatusChanged:Connect(function(isRunning) print(isRunning and "started" or "stopped") end) Becomes true when running count reaches 0, and false when it goes above 0.

searchDocs.title: LuauRan​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.LuauRan: ScriptSignal<boolean,{any}> Fires after LuauDispatcher.run AST execution. Dispatcher.LuauRan:Connect(function(success, result) if success then print("success") else print("failed") end end) result is the AST that was executed, or was attempted to be executed.

searchDocs.title: UnhandledReturn​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.UnhandledReturn: ScriptSignal<...any> Fires when a script returns at the top level (essentially returning a value to nothing) Dispatcher.UnhandledReturn:Connect(function(...) print("returned", select("#", ...), "value(s):", ...) end) local options = { ... } local success, tree = LuauParser.parse("return 1", options) Dispatcher.run(tree) -- Output: returned 1 value(s): 1

searchDocs.title: Errored​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.Errored: ScriptSignal<any> Fires whenever a script throws an error. It is recommended to disable allowConsoleOutput in RuntimeOptions when using this connection. The function that gets connected will receive the error message

searchDocs.title: Output​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.Output: ScriptSignal<...any> Fires whenever a script calls print. The function that gets connected will receive any argument passed to the print function Dispatcher.Output:Connect(function(...) print(...) -- This code would actually be redundant, but it demonstrates the functionality end)

searchDocs.title: Warned​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.Warned: ScriptSignal<...any> Like LuauDispatcher.Output, but for warn.

searchDocs.title: LuauError​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.LuauError: LuauError The error-formatting module the Dispatcher uses. You can call Dispatcher.LuauError.isErrors() on an AST to check if it has any issues.

searchDocs.title: globals​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.globals: {[string]: any} Add your own globals here (before running) to give them to scripts: Dispatcher.globals.MyApi = MyApi You can also replace any globals, if you want to do that for some reason. This will not work if trying to replace a console-printing function, such as warn, print, error, etc. You should instead connect to LuauDispatcher.Output, LuauDispatcher.Warned, or LuauDispatcher.Errored. A script can also call the rawglobal(name, value) function to set a global that will stay in this table. This can be disabled when running Dispatcher.run() by passing in an options table.

searchDocs.title: exprs​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.exprs: {[string]: ( self: Runtime, node: any, scope: Scope ) → ...any} Registry of expression handlers. If you'd like to play with these, change them, or add more, you might want to better understand the Scope library.

searchDocs.title: stats​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.stats: {[string]: ( self: Runtime, node: any, scope: Scope ) → Completion} Registry of statement handlers

searchDocs.title: run​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.run( tree: any,-- a parsed tree from LuauParser options: RuntimeOptions?, ...: any-- Varargs passed to the script ) → Completion Executes a tree produced by LuauParser. Returns a Completion describing how the script finished: kind is "Normal" for a script that just ran, or "Return" if the script returned. Alternatively you can specifically monitor for returns by connecting to LuauDispatcher.UnhandledReturn. Optionally, you can pass in an options table of type RuntimeOptions to enable or disable specific parser features. Default options: local runtimeOptions = { allowEnvironmentModifications = true, rawGlobalEnabled = true, -- this might be the one you consider disabling overrideCertainGlobalsAtRuntime = true, instructionLimit = 10000000, noEnv = false, allowConsoleOutput = true, } You may also pass in arguments to the script by passing them in after the options table. Ex. local success, tree = LuauParser.parse([[ local msg1, msg2 = ... print(msg1.." "..msg2) ]]) local completion = Dispatcher.run(tree, {}, "Hello", "World")

searchDocs.title: evaluateNode​
searchDocs.content: </> LuauDispatcher.evaluateNode( node: AstNode,-- any single AST node scope: Scope?,-- an optional scope to evaluate within options: RuntimeOptions? ) → Completion | ...any Evaluates a single node. CAUTION Prefer LuauDispatcher.run whenever possible. Unlike LuauDispatcher.run, this does not automatically check the tree for parse errors, wrap execution in a pcall, update LuauDispatcher.RunningCount, or fire RunningStatusChanged, LuauRan, or UnhandledReturn
